title: Rail Fence Cipher Part 2: Pattern Recognition
body:
When a Rail Fence Cipher uses more than two rails, the zig-zag pattern becomes wider and takes longer to repeat. These longer patterns can leave clues in the ciphertext about how the letters were arranged, even if you don't decode the full message. 
In this lesson, you'll look for structural clues that suggest the cipher used more than two rails and consider how the letters might fit into a longer zig-zag path.
lesson: CRYPTO-D1A-9f5a2f70, CRYPTO-D1B-55c1a1de, CRYPTO-D4A-8b7f12c9, CRYPTO-D4B-74ad0c21, CRYPTO-D5A-90c3e4e2, CRYPTO-D5B-1f0a76bd
